tasks.title: Shared Button component library — extract from 57+ primary + 65+ ghost ad-hoc button patterns
tasks.description:
Create a reusable `<Button>` component and convert the highest-traffic button patterns:

**1. Create Button component** — Create `apps/web/src/components/shared/Button.tsx` with variants: `primary` (bg-brand-green text-white hover:bg-brand-green-hover), `secondary` (border border-slate-300 text-slate-700 hover:bg-slate-50), `ghost` (text-slate-600 hover:text-slate-800, no bg/border), `danger` (bg-red-500 text-white hover:bg-red-600 OR text-red-600 hover:text-red-800 for ghost-danger). Props: `variant` (default 'primary'), `size` ('sm' | 'md' | 'lg', default 'md'), `disabled`, `loading` (shows spinner), `className` (for overrides), plus all native button HTML attributes via `React.ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement>`. Sizes: `sm` = `px-3 py-1 text-xs`, `md` = `px-4 py-1.5 text-sm`, `lg` = `px-5 py-2 text-sm`. All variants include `disabled:opacity-50 disabled:cursor-not-allowed` and `transition-colors`. Export as default.

**2. Convert primary buttons in key modals** — Replace ad-hoc primary button classes in the 10 highest-traffic components with `<Button>`: `ProjectSettingsModal.tsx` (5 buttons), `TaskPlanApprovalDialog.tsx` (4 buttons), `SprintPlanModal.tsx` (2 buttons), `CloseSprintModal.tsx` (4 buttons), `PRDBreakdownModal.tsx` (2 buttons). Import Button and replace the `<button className="px-3 py-1.5 bg-brand-green text-white text-sm rounded hover:bg-brand-green-hover disabled:opacity-50">` pattern with `<Button size="sm">`. Keep the same visual result.

**3. Convert ghost buttons in ProjectDetail** — `apps/web/src/pages/ProjectDetail.tsx` has ~19 ghost-style toolbar buttons. Replace the most repetitive patterns (the action toolbar buttons like Summarize, Health, Trends, Notes, etc.) with `<Button variant="ghost" size="sm">`. Don't replace one-off styled buttons or links.
acceptance: Button.tsx exists with primary, secondary, ghost, danger variants
Button supports sm, md, lg sizes with correct padding/font
Button supports loading prop with spinner
Button extends React.ButtonHTMLAttributes for native props
At least 15 ad-hoc buttons converted to <Button> across the listed files
Visual output matches previous ad-hoc styles exactly
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build all pass

tasks.title: Dark mode infrastructure — Tailwind config + CSS vars + base component dark: variants
tasks.description:
Set up dark mode infrastructure so it can be toggled when ready, without a visible toggle yet:

**1. Tailwind dark mode config** — In `apps/web/tailwind.config.js`, add `darkMode: 'class'` to enable class-based dark mode (adding `dark` class to `<html>` activates it). This is the standard approach for user-preference-based toggling.

**2. Dark mode CSS custom properties** — In `apps/web/src/index.css`, add a `.dark` selector block with dark variants of the brand colors: `--brand-green: #66BB6A` (slightly lighter for dark bg), `--brand-green-hover: #4CAF50`, `--brand-green-light: #1B3A1B` (dark green tint instead of light), `--brand-dark: #0F1A24` (even darker). This means brand token classes automatically adapt when dark mode is active.

**3. Base layout dark: variants** — Add `dark:` Tailwind classes to the core layout elements only (not every component — just the shell):
- `apps/web/src/pages/AppLayout.tsx`: main content area `bg-slate-50` → add `dark:bg-slate-900`, sidebar `bg-slate-800` → add `dark:bg-brand-dark`, text colors on sidebar
- `apps/web/src/pages/Login.tsx` / `Signup.tsx`: page bg `bg-slate-100` → add `dark:bg-slate-900`, card `bg-white` → add `dark:bg-slate-800`, text colors
- `apps/web/src/pages/Home.tsx`: same pattern for the centered layout
- `apps/web/src/components/shared/Button.tsx` (just created in task-001): add dark: variants to each variant style
- `apps/web/src/components/shared/Modal.tsx`: backdrop and modal card dark variants

Do NOT add dark: to every component — just the outermost shell and the two shared components (Button, Modal). The rest will be done incrementally. Add a comment in tailwind.config.js: `// To activate: add 'dark' class to <html> element`.
acceptance: tailwind.config.js has darkMode: 'class'
index.css has .dark block with adjusted brand color variables
AppLayout has dark: variants on main bg, sidebar
Login, Signup, Home pages have dark: variants on page bg and card
Button.tsx and Modal.tsx have dark: variants
Adding 'dark' class to <html> visually switches to dark theme on layout shell
Light mode is completely unchanged (no visual regression)
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build all pass

tasks.title: Integration test foundation — test database setup + auth resolver tests
tasks.description:
Set up integration test infrastructure and write the first resolver tests:

**1. Test database setup** — Create `apps/api/src/__tests__/setup.integration.ts` that: (a) creates a test Prisma client pointing to a test database (use `DATABASE_URL` from env or default to `postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/tasktoad_test`), (b) runs `prisma migrate deploy` or `prisma db push` to ensure schema is up to date, (c) provides a `cleanDatabase()` function that truncates all tables between tests (use `prisma.$executeRaw` with TRUNCATE CASCADE), (d) exports the test prisma client and cleanup utilities. Create `apps/api/vitest.config.integration.ts` that extends the base config but only runs `*.integration.test.ts` files. Add a `test:integration` script to `apps/api/package.json`: `"test:integration": "vitest run --config vitest.config.integration.ts"`.

**2. Auth resolver integration tests** — Create `apps/api/src/__tests__/auth.integration.test.ts` with tests that hit the actual Prisma database:
- `signup` — creates user in DB, verify password is hashed (not plaintext), verify user record exists with correct email
- `signup` duplicate email — returns ConflictError
- `signup` short password — returns ValidationError
- `login` — returns valid JWT token, verify token can be decoded with jose
- `login` wrong password — returns AuthenticationError
- `login` nonexistent email — returns AuthenticationError
- `createOrg` — creates org, attaches user as admin

For each test, build a mock `Context` object with the test Prisma client and a mock user (for authenticated operations). Use `beforeEach` to clean the database. Tests should call the resolver functions directly (import from `resolvers/auth.ts`), not through HTTP/GraphQL yoga.

**3. Root integration test script** — Add `"test:integration": "pnpm --filter api test:integration"` to root `package.json`.
acceptance: Test database setup creates/connects to tasktoad_test database
cleanDatabase() truncates all tables between tests
Auth tests cover: signup success, duplicate email, short password, login success, wrong password, nonexistent email
Tests call resolver functions directly with mock Context
pnpm --filter api test:integration runs and all tests pass (requires running Postgres)
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build all pass

tasks.title: TypeScript strictness — add Zod validation to 7 unvalidated JSON.parse calls + fix as unknown casts
tasks.description:
Improve type safety by adding Zod validation to all unvalidated JSON.parse calls in the API:

**1. Create shared Zod schemas** — In `apps/api/src/utils/zodSchemas.ts` (new file), define reusable schemas: `StringArraySchema = z.array(z.string())` (for columns, events, statuses), `TriggerConditionSchema` (for automation triggers — already exists in projectrole.ts, move to shared location), `ActionSchema` (for automation actions). Export all schemas.

**2. Add Zod validation to 7 unvalidated JSON.parse calls:**
- `apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/sprint.ts:221` — `JSON.parse(target.columns) as string[]` → use `StringArraySchema.parse(JSON.parse(target.columns))`
- `apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/task.ts:116,156` — `JSON.parse(project.statuses) as string[]` → same pattern
- `apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/ai.ts:538` — `JSON.parse(args.epics)` → add appropriate Zod schema for epics input
- `apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/slack.ts:206` — `JSON.parse(parent.events) as string[]` → `StringArraySchema.parse()`
- `apps/api/src/utils/automationEngine.ts:42,57` — `JSON.parse(rule.trigger/action)` → use TriggerConditionSchema and ActionSchema
- `apps/api/src/utils/notificationUtils.ts:35` — `JSON.parse(int.events) as string[]` → `StringArraySchema.parse()`
- `apps/api/src/utils/webhookDispatcher.ts:133` — `JSON.parse(ep.events) as string[]` → `StringArraySchema.parse()`

For each, wrap in try-catch: if Zod validation fails, log a warning and use a safe fallback (empty array for string arrays, skip execution for triggers/actions). Do NOT throw to the user — these are internal data that should always be valid, and a Zod error means data corruption that shouldn't crash the request.

**3. Fix `as unknown` casts** — In `apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/task.ts:592-593` and `search.ts:34`, the `as unknown as { ... }` casts are covering for missing Prisma include types. Fix by adding the correct `include` clause to the Prisma query and letting the type flow naturally. In `export.ts:214,311`, the `rows as unknown as Record<>` casts for CSV may need a proper type for the csv-stringify input.
acceptance: zodSchemas.ts exists with StringArraySchema and shared automation schemas
All 7 unvalidated JSON.parse calls now use Zod .parse() or .safeParse()
Zod failures log warnings and use safe fallbacks (don't throw to user)
as unknown casts in task.ts and search.ts eliminated via correct Prisma includes
No new any types introduced
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build all pass

tasks.title: AI code review UI — review button on TaskDetailPanel + results display for in-review tasks
tasks.description:
The backend for AI code review is fully implemented (`reviewPullRequest` mutation, `reviewCode` in aiService, `getPullRequestDiff` in GitHub service). This task adds the frontend UI:

**1. AI Review button** — In `apps/web/src/components/TaskDetailPanel.tsx`, add an "AI Review" button in the task detail toolbar area (near the existing generate instructions button, around line 234). The button should be visible when: (a) the task has a linked PR (`task.pullRequests.length > 0`), AND (b) the task status is `in_review` or the user clicks manually. Show the button as `<Button variant="secondary" size="sm">AI Review</Button>` (using the new Button component if available, otherwise ad-hoc). When clicked, call the `reviewPullRequest` mutation with the task's first PR number.

**2. Review results display** — Create `apps/web/src/components/taskdetail/TaskAIReviewSection.tsx` that displays the code review results. Props: `review: CodeReview | null`, `loading: boolean`. Layout: (a) approval badge — green checkmark or red X with "Approved" / "Changes Requested", (b) summary paragraph, (c) comments list — each with file path, line number, severity (info/warning/error), and message, (d) suggestions list — each with title and description. Use the existing card/section styling pattern from other taskdetail sections (similar to TaskDependenciesSection). Add a `CodeReview` type to `apps/web/src/types.ts`: `{ summary: string; approved: boolean; comments: Array<{ file: string; line: number; severity: string; message: string }>; suggestions: Array<{ title: string; description: string }> }`.

**3. Wire into useTaskCRUD** — In `apps/web/src/hooks/useTaskCRUD.ts`, add a `handleReviewPR` function that calls the `reviewPullRequest(taskId, prNumber)` mutation and returns the CodeReview result. Add state for `reviewResult` and `reviewLoading`. Pass these through to TaskDetailPanel.
acceptance: AI Review button visible on tasks with linked PRs
Clicking button calls reviewPullRequest mutation with correct taskId and prNumber
Loading state shown while review is in progress
Review results display: approval badge, summary, comments with file/line, suggestions
CodeReview type added to types.ts
handleReviewPR function added to useTaskCRUD
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build all pass

tasks.title: Enhanced API documentation — interactive schema explorer with auth examples and rate limit docs
tasks.description:
Enhance the existing `/api/docs` route from basic HTML to a comprehensive, interactive API reference:

**1. Improve docs.ts** — In `apps/api/src/routes/docs.ts`, enhance the auto-generated HTML page to be a proper API reference. The current implementation extracts queries/mutations from SDL and renders them as code blocks. Improve it to:
- Group operations by domain (Auth, Project, Task, Sprint, Comment, AI, Search, Notification, Slack, Webhook, GitHub)
- For each operation, show: name, description (extract from SDL comments if present), arguments with types, return type
- Add an "Authentication" section at the top explaining: Bearer token in Authorization header, how to get a token via login mutation, token expiration (7 days)
- Add a "Rate Limits" section documenting: global (200/min), auth (10/min for signup/login), sensitive (5/min for password reset), export (5/10min), SSE (5 concurrent)
- Add a "Quick Start" section with curl examples for: login, create project, create task, list tasks
- Style the page with the brand colors (brand-green for headers, brand-dark for code blocks)
- Add a sidebar/table of contents for navigation

**2. Schema download endpoint** — Add `GET /api/docs/schema.graphql` that returns the raw SDL text (using `printSchema()` which is already imported). This lets external tools consume the schema.

**3. JSON schema endpoint** — Add `GET /api/docs/schema.json` that returns the introspection result as JSON (use `graphql`'s `introspectionFromSchema()` or execute the standard introspection query). This enables tools like Postman, Insomnia, or graphql-codegen to auto-import the schema.
acceptance: GET /api/docs shows operations grouped by domain with arguments and return types
Authentication section with Bearer token instructions and curl examples
Rate limits section documenting all rate limit tiers
Quick Start section with working curl examples
GET /api/docs/schema.graphql returns raw SDL text
GET /api/docs/schema.json returns introspection JSON
Page styled with brand colors and navigable via sidebar/TOC
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build all pass
